title: Bow (clone stormtrooper)

{{Quote|Execute the enemies of the Empire.|Appo, ordering Bow and the other 501st clone stormtroopers to aid Darth Vader on Kessel|<ref name="Purge">[[Purge (comic book)|''Purge'']]</ref>}}
'''Bow''' was the name of a [[clone stormtrooper]] who served as a [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|commander]] within the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]]. A loyal and obedient [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], he rose to his [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] by around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] used to brand all [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] as traitors to the Empire and initiate the [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-wide [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Jedi Purge]]. One [[Standard month/Legends|month]] into the purge, Bow was part of a 501st [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Squad/Legends|squad]] that [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]] [[Appo/Legends|Appo]] took to [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. There, Bow and his fellow [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] [[Conclave on Kessel|rescued]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] from a batch of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who had survived Order 66. Bow and his comrades gunned down [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Jastus Farr]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], and [[Roblio Darté]], [[Death/Legends|executing]] them in mid-battle with Vader. Following the incident, Bow personally filed [[Casualty Report: Order 66|reports]] on several of the Jedi victims, and he continued to serve in the 501st after Appo's death at the [[Battle of Kashyyyk (19 BBY)|Battle of Kashyyyk]].

==Biography==
The [[clone stormtrooper]] who took on the name Bow<ref name="CSWE: Bow">{{CSWECite|I|90|Bow, Commander}}</ref> was [[Cloning/Legends|born]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]].<ref name="Encyclopedia: Clones" /> By [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Purge" /> he had gained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Stormtrooper Commander/Legends|commander]] within the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]],<ref name="O66" /> one of the most decorated [[Legion/Legends|units]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] newly founded [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

[[File:501stSquad-Purge.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Bow and the rest of Appo's squad arrive to save Vader on Kessel.]]
One [[Standard month/Legends|month]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the Empire,<ref name="Purge" /> Bow was part of<ref name="CSWE: Bow" /> a 501st [[Squad/Legends|squad]] that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] sent to [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]],<ref name="Purge" /> a planet in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Serving under [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commander]]<ref name="Hasbro: Appo">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Saga Collection|pack=#064: Commander Appo|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TSC064appo.asp}}</ref> [[Appo/Legends|Appo]], the overall [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]] of the 501st Legion<ref name="O66" />—Bow and the other clone [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in the squad received orders to retrieve Palpatine's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Vader had fallen afoul of a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who had survived the issuance of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], a [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|directive]] that Palpatine had used to brand all [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] as enemies to the Empire and launch the ongoing [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Jedi Purge]].<ref name="Purge" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] Bow and his comrades arrived at the [[Unidentified Kessel spice mine|abandoned spice mine]] on Kessel where the Jedi renegades had [[Conclave on Kessel|gathered]], Vader was severely injured and at the mercy of the three surviving Jedi. From their position on a platform overlooking the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|mine]], Bow and the other [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] drew their [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15Ss]] and gunned down [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Masters]] [[Jastus Farr]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], and [[Roblio Darté]], [[Death/Legends|executing]] all three before they could further harm Vader. With the standoff concluded, Appo informed Vader that the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] had requested his presence. Vader subsequently returned to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], to report to Palpatine.<ref name="Purge" />

After the Kessel incident, Bow filed reports on the termination of Choi and two of the other Jedi victims, [[Koffi Arana]] and [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]], who had fallen prior to the 501st's arrival.<ref name="O66" /> Later,<ref name="Hasbro: Appo" /> when Vader killed Jedi Master [[Roan Shryne]]—who had decapitated Appo before being murdered—at the [[Battle of Kashyyyk (19 BBY)|Battle of Kashyyyk]],<ref name="Dark Lord">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref> Bow also filed the report on Shryne's execution. Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Marcellin Wessel]] incorporated Bow's official documentations into a [[Casualty Report: Order 66|compilation of incident reports]] on the success of Order 66 and the continuing Jedi Purge.<ref name="O66" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:BowAndVader-RBS.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Bow and his commanding officer, Darth Vader]]
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] clone of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], Bow stood 1.83 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="Encyclopedia: Clones" /> A loyal [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]], he obeyed [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader's orders to the letter and without question in his service to the Galactic Empire.<ref name="Hasbro: Vader & Bow">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|text=''Star Wars: Order 66'' (Series 1)|pack=#3: Darth Vader & Commander Bow|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tacOrder663.asp}}</ref>

==Equipment==
Bow sported the trademark [[Color/Legends|blue]]-and-white [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|armor]] of the 501st Legion. His set of stormtrooper [[Armor/Legends|armor]] was equipped with a [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]] visor, a [[Kama/Legends|''kama'']] half kilt, and a [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldron]]. He also had [[Holster/Legends|holsters]] for his weaponry of choice: a chest sling for his [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]] and a pair of waist holsters for his dual [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]].<ref name="Hasbro: Vader & Bow" /> During the mission to Kessel, he carried a DC-15S [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]].<ref name="Purge" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] Bow was created by author [[Daniel Wallace]] for his ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article ''[[Order 66: Destroy All Jedi]]'', released in [[April]] [[2006]] in the magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 87|eighty-seventh issue]].<ref name="O66" /> Wallace introduced the character because he needed another clone officer to report on the death of [[Appo|Commander Appo]], who had already registered several reports within the [[in-universe]] article.<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23620481|board=Literature|thread="Order 66 Destroy All Jedi" by Daniel Wallace (SWI #87)|user=[[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]]|date=2006-04-09|time=8:19 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200418183101/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/order-66-destroy-all-jedi-by-daniel-wallace-swi-87.23608826/page-2}}</ref> In [[2007]], Bow was featured in his own figure pack—alongside Darth Vader—of [[Hasbro]]'s [[Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|''30th Anniversary Collection'']].<ref name="Hasbro: Vader & Bow" />

===The commander in ''Purge''===
{{Quote|A clone commander who saved Darth Vader from being killed by Jedi Knights on Kessel after the events of Order 66.|Bow's brief entry in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'', which establishes him as one of the clone stormtroopers in ''Purge'' alongside Commander Appo|<ref name="The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>}}
For some time, there was ambiguity as to the identity of the clone stormtrooper commander who leads the 501st squad in the [[December]] [[2005]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']]. The character goes unnamed within the comic's dialogue, hence leaving his identity ambiguous, with the possibility of the unidentified character being Appo, Bow, or another 501st commander. Further complicating the problem was the fact that, at the time, ''Purge'' did not have an established timeline in relation to the [[November]] 2005 novel ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'', in which Appo is killed; both ''Purge'' and ''Dark Lord'' are stated to take place one month after the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="Purge" /><ref name="Dark Lord" /> The ''Purge'' commander's identity was resolved in 2006 when Hasbro released a figure of Appo as part of its [[Star Wars: The Saga Collection|''Saga Collection'']]. The figure package identified Appo as the commander in charge of the 501st squad in the comic.<ref name="Hasbro: Appo" />

In December [[2008]], Bow received a short entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which [[Retroactive continuity|established]] that he appears in ''Purge'' himself as one of the clone stormtroopers<ref name="CSWE: Bow" /> serving under Appo.<ref name="Hasbro: Appo" /> However, the exact trooper in the comic who is Bow has yet to be specified.
